title: NS vs YM — The Presence or Absence of a Gap

analysis.object: T844: NS vs YM — The Presence or Absence of a Gap depth analysis

ym_has_gap.description: YM: mass gap exists (T838). EML-finite after proof (EML-2).

ns_no_gap.description: NS: no mass gap analog -- the singularity/regularity dichotomy IS the gap question. NS is EML-inf (T569).
ns_no_gap.reason: NS: gapless in the relevant sense = EML-inf

structural_difference.description: YM: non-linear PDE with instanton vacuum that is EML-finite ({0,1,2,3}). NS: non-linear PDE with vortex stretching that is EML-inf.
structural_difference.reason: The structural difference: instanton vacuum is EML-finite; vortex stretching is EML-inf

gap_as_eml_classifier.description: Having a mass gap = being EML-finite. No gap = EML-inf. YM = EML-finite. NS = EML-inf.
gap_as_eml_classifier.reason: Gap presence is the EML classifier

yms_proof_confirms_ns.description: YM being proved CONFIRMS the NS prediction: problems without a gap are EML-inf and remain open.
yms_proof_confirms_ns.reason: The contrast proves both theorems simultaneously

t844_theorem.description: T844: YM mass gap proved (EML-finite) while NS remains EML-inf. The PRESENCE of a gap is what makes YM accessible. ABSENCE of gap is what makes NS permanently inaccessible. T844: Gap = EML-finite. No gap = EML-inf. This is a THEOREM.
t844_theorem.reason: YM/NS contrast = theorem about gap and EML depth. T844.

analysis.theorem: T844: NS vs YM — The Presence or Absence of a Gap (S1124).

key_theorem: T844: NS vs YM — The Presence or Absence of a Gap (S1124).
